title: skopeo copy has mismatched digest and layer information
body:
I'm trying to use _skopeo copy_ to manually promote images between two different OKD/OpenShift environments. While the operation itself works I've noticed that the image layers and digest info differ between registries. Issue #393 explains the duplicated layer names but I'm not sure it explains why these "non-opp" layers only appear on the destination side of the copy (see output below). I was under the impression that _skopeo copy_ was a bit for bit copy from one location to another without format transformations etc so I'd like to understand why the different digests :)

Some selected output from _skopeo inspect_ from each side:


comments.body:
Some other data:
- Source registry is the internal registry included with OKD 3.11
- Destination registry is whatever is currently included with OpenShift Online v3

Hi @patrick-tavares,

thanks for reaching out and for providing all the details. The critical part of why we see the empty layers is shown in the logs:
```
DEBU[0001] Uploading empty layer during conversion to schema 1
```

The source image is in the docker 2 schema 2 format while the destination registry seems to only support schema 1 images. This requires to convert from schema 2 to schema 1 where empty layers are represented as an actual empty layer yielding to the this confusing representation. Schema 2 is vastly different than schema 1 and allows to properly represent empty layers.

Thanks for the reply @vrothberg. I missed that empty layers line but did see the schema conversion. Looks like OpenShift Online isn't yet running the registry with schema 2 support :( I guess the new empty layers would also explain the difference in digests.

Thanks again!
